title: Bridge Essay: Pain perception is not modulated by the rubber hand illusion in healthy participants: Insights from a systematic literature review and meta-analysis.
concept: Body as process not substance — neuroplasticity reveals the self-model as continuously updated prediction.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Physical De-solidification** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Body as process not substance — neuroplasticity reveals the self-model as continuously updated prediction.

**Pain perception is not modulated by the rubber hand illusion in healthy participants: Insights from a systematic literature review and meta-analysis.** - Neuroscience and biobehavioral reviews (2026). DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2026.106770. PMID: 42176763. Viewing a prosthetic hand stroked at the same time and in the same place as a real hand hidden behind a screen can create the sense that the visible hand is one's own. This phenomenon, known as the rubber hand illusion (RHI), is a widely used paradigm to study the sense of body ownership. It has been hypothesized that RHI attenuates pain by altering body representation, which may in turn influence pain processing. However, the empirical findings remain inconsistent.
